Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First let me understand the activities 1 RO Receive Order 2 CA Check Availability 3 RI Reserve Items 4 PI Pick Items 5 QC Quality Check 6 PO Pack Order 7 GSL Generate Shipping Label 8 D Dispatch 9 IC Invoice Customer 10 RP Record Payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process Flow Analysis RO is the start CA follows RO checking availability comes after receiving RI follows CA reserve only after checking availability PI follows RI pick after reserving QC follows PI check quality after picking PO follows QC pack after quality check GSL follows PO generate label after packing D follows GSL dispatch after label generation IC typically happens around dispatch time or after RP happens after IC payment recorded after invoicing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Equivalence constraints Are there any activities that must occur the same number of times Maybe RO and D if order is received it should be dispatched Better GSL and D one label per dispatch IC and RP might be equivalent if payment is always recorded for each invoice

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually looking at standard process modeling equivalence is quite restrictive I will add GSL and D as equivalent and maybe CA and RI if we assume one to one mapping But I should be careful

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before if first occurs second occurred before RI CA if RI occurs CA happened before PI RI if PI occurs RI happened before QC PI if QC occurs PI happened before PO QC if PO occurs QC happened before GSL PO if GSL occurs PO happened before D GSL if D occurs GSL happened before RP IC if RP occurs IC happened before CA RO if CA occurs RO happened before it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After RO CA if RO occurs CA occurs later CA RI if CA occurs RI occurs later RI PI if RI occurs PI occurs later PI QC if PI occurs QC occurs later QC PO if QC occurs PO occurs later PO GSL if PO occurs GSL occurs later GSL D if GSL occurs D occurs later D IC if D occurs IC occurs later IC RP if IC occurs RP occurs later

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Never Together Are there any mutually exclusive activities In the standard flow no I will set Never Together to empty set

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences Let me define RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 2 QC 1 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 0 1

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence PI QC if picking occurs then number of PI equals number of QC This makes sense if every picked batch is quality checked PO GSL if packing occurs number of PO equals number of GSL GSL D if label generated number of GSL equals number of D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to check the directly follows logic Is it realistic to have IC immediately followed by RP Probably not as payment takes time Also is QC immediately followed by PO Maybe PO to GSL to D are likely immediate

Name: Validation - PE
Text: One final check Are there any constraints that contradict Activ freq for RP is 0 1 but Always After IC RP means if IC occurs RP occurs after Since IC occurs RP must occur So RP cannot be 0

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: So I should either remove IC RP from Always After or change RP to 1 Given the scenario says once the customer pays I should remove IC RP from Always After

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Revised Always After without IC RP RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC This looks consistent

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final dictionary uses equivalence PI QC PO GSL GSL D always before CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC always after RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC never together empty activ freq RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 2 QC 1 2 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 0 1 directly follows RO CA CA RI RI PI QC PO PO GSL GSL D
